{{Quote|According to legend, the Jedi Knights built the space station Nespis 8 as a meeting place for scientists from across the galaxy. The station was devoted to knowledge and learning, and it was considered neutral territory. Even if two planets were involved in a brutal war, their scientists could come to Nespis to do research. As knowledge grew, so did the station, until it was supposed to have grown to the size of a small planet. The legends say that Nespis 8 contained all the knowledge in the galaxy. Including, all the wisdom of the Jedi.|[[DV-9]], to [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash Arranda]]|<ref name="GotJ" />}}
'''Nespis VIII''', or '''Nespis 8''', also known as the '''Nespis VIII spaceport''' or '''Space City''', was a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in the [[Auril system/Legends|Auril system]] constructed sometime during the dawn of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==History==
[[File:AurilSystemNespisVIIIBase.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Nespis VIII]]
The orbiting city was constructed during the early days of hyperspace travel as an isolated interstellar beacon. As of [[10,000 BBY]] the City was listed by [[Vicendi]] as one of the [[Twenty Wonders of the Galaxy]]. It lasted through thousands of years of galactic conflict, including the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Sith War]] and the destruction of the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]], but was largely abandoned by the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="EA" /> As a result, the station was filled with ancient relics from every era of space travel. A repository of Jedi records was located on the station, but [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] destroyed this library and killed its guardian, [[Aidan Bok]] during the [[Great Jedi Purge]]. Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Borborygmus Gog]] created a false library in place of the original, hoping to lure and capture [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] subjects for his studies.<ref name="GotJ" />

Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Mammon Hoole]] and the Arranda children sought refuge on the station with [[Borborygmus Gog|ForceFlow]], a [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] friend of [[Tash Arranda/Legends|Tash Arranda's]]. ForceFlow tricked Hoole and the Arrandas into joining the many artifact prospectors searching for the legendary Jedi library. Tash Arranda located the library, and the spirit of [[Aidan Bok]], who warned her of Gog's artifice. ForceFlow subsequently revealed himself to be Borborygmus Gog, but with the help of Aidan Bok, Tash escaped the Imperial scientist.<ref name="GotJ" />

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]] met there, and Luke brought Kam back to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]. [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] leadership fled to the space station after the destruction of [[Pinnacle Base]] on [[Da Soocha V]], and it became the birthplace of [[Anakin Solo]].<ref name="DEII">''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]''</ref> It was armed with [[V-200 ion cannon]]s by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], during the events surrounding [[Operation Shadow Hand]] in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="EE" />

A traitor within the New Republic soldiery leaked Mothma's location to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. However, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] alerted his masters to this betrayal, and the New Republic began evacuation immediately. Attempting to slaughter the Alliance leaders wholesale before they could escape, the reborn Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ordered the launch of a [[Galaxy Gun]] projectile to destroy the ancient space city. Although New Republic starfighters failed to deter the massive missile, a faulty timing mechanism manufactured on [[Roonadan]] prevented the weapon's detonation. The enraged Emperor fired a second projectile, but the bulk of the Alliance fleet made hyperspace just as the space station exploded.<ref name="EE" />
